title: Clibadium asperum
intro: Clibadium asperum, also known as Clibadium, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Clibadium asperum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Clibadium asperum is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat fever, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Clibadium asperum is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. The genus Clibadium belongs to the order Apiales. Clibadium asperum is native to Central America. This plant is a member of the subfamily Aralioideae. Clibadium asperum is closely related to other species within the genus Clibadium.
